content: I'll split this bead into 5 sequential child beads, each focused on one part of the test pipeline.

args.command:
br create "Add test dispatch endpoint to bypass Web Speech API" "## Child of: adc-50m6

Create a new test endpoint POST /api/v1/test/dispatch that accepts raw utterance text directly, bypassing the Web Speech API microphone input.

### Requirements
- Endpoint accepts JSON body with {utterance, session_id, surface_id}
- Session ID and surface ID work the same as /dispatch
- Utterance is plain text (no audio/transcription step)
- Returns the same response format as /dispatch

### Acceptance Criteria
- Endpoint exists at POST /api/v1/test/dispatch
- Accepts utterance text directly in JSON body
- Bypasses Web Speech API completely
- Returns 200 on valid input" --label split-child
args.description: Create child bead 1: test dispatch endpoint

args.command:
br create "Verify intent classification works via test endpoint" "## Child of: adc-50m6

Verify that the test endpoint correctly routes utterances through the intent classifier and produces the same classification results as the main /dispatch endpoint.

### Requirements
- Test endpoint calls the same intent router as /dispatch
- Classification produces identical results for identical utterances
- No microphone/audio layer interference

### Acceptance Criteria
- Test utterance 'what is the weather' → classified as weather intent
- Test utterance 'tell me about X' → classified as research intent
- Classification matches /dispatch behavior for the same inputs" --label split-child
args.description: Create child bead 2: intent classification verification

args.command:
br create "Verify fetch strands execute via test endpoint" "## Child of: adc-50m6

Verify that fetch strands execute correctly when triggered through the test endpoint, producing the same fetch results as the main dispatch flow.

### Requirements
- Test endpoint triggers fetch orchestration
- All configured fetch strands run
- Results match those from /dispatch

### Acceptance Criteria
- Weather fetch executes and returns data
- Research fetch executes and returns data
- Fetch results are identical to /dispatch for same utterance
- No fetch strand is skipped or fails" --label split-child
args.description: Create child bead 3: fetch strands verification

args.command:
br create "Verify storage and SSE broadcast via test endpoint" "## Child of: adc-50m6

Verify that results from the test endpoint are correctly stored in the session database and broadcast via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.

### Requirements
- Results persist to SQLite session store
- SSE events broadcast to correct surfaces
- Broadcast timing matches /dispatch

### Acceptance Criteria
- Result stored in data/session.db
- SSE event with type='result_created' broadcast
- Canvas receives event at surface_id
- Storage payload matches /dispatch payload" --label split-child
args.description: Create child bead 4: storage and SSE verification

args.command:
br create "Verify canvas renders test endpoint results correctly" "## Child of: adc-50m6

Verify that the canvas UI correctly receives and renders result cards from the test endpoint, completing the full pipeline test.

### Requirements
- Canvas fetches topics from /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics
- Cards render with correct content
- Visual output matches /dispatch results

### Acceptance Criteria
- Canvas displays result cards from test dispatch
- Card content matches fetch results
- No visual differences between test and real dispatch
- SSE triggers canvas refresh on result_created" --label split-child
args.description: Create child bead 5: canvas rendering verification

Add a dependency

Usage: br dep add [OPTIONS] <BLOCKER>

Arguments:
  <BLOCKER>  Bead that blocks (the bead being depended on)

Options:
      --blocks <BLOCKS>        Bead that is blocked (depends on the blocker)
  -w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>  Workspace directory (defaults to current directory's .beads/)
  -t, --type <TYPE>            Dependency type (e.g., blocks, relates_to) [default: blocks]
  -h, --help                   Print help
